title: Partial Clone, Sparse Checkout, and Large-Repository Scale · Graph Lens: Gita Sparse Cone Noncone
summary: A multi-gigabyte monorepo has slow clone and status operations, developers need different cones, CI requires complete release objects, and an unreliable promisor remote may disappear. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita sparse cone noncone increment in large-repository data and working-set contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use cone patterns, non-cone patterns, set, add, reapply, disable, skip-worktree state, and sparse index while detecting vivified paths.
Compare shallow clone, partial clone, sparse checkout, sparse index, single branch, and reference repositories by omitted data and semantic limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Sparse Cone Noncone
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G15 is a theory in a disposable repository. the failing build omits a required shared package from the declared cone. The learner must predict the active sparse paths expose the intended working set without claiming object completeness, run git sparse-checkout list, explain apps/status is present while packages/shared is absent from the build working set, test this changed condition: add packages/shared and repeat both sparse-index status and full-object release verification, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the prior sparse specification and support disabling sparse checkout reversibly.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Sparse Cone Noncone contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use cone patterns, non-cone patterns, set, add, reapply, disable, skip-worktree state, and sparse index while detecting vivified paths.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-sparse-cone-noncone — Use cone patterns, non-cone patterns, set, add, reapply, disable, skip-worktree state, and sparse index while detecting vivified paths.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the failing build omits a required shared package from the declared cone
git sparse-checkout list
# expected: the active sparse paths expose the intended working set without claiming object completeness
# observation: apps/status is present while packages/shared is absent from the build working set
# changed-case: add packages/shared and repeat both sparse-index status and full-object release verification
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: retain the prior sparse specification and support disabling sparse checkout reversibly
# transfer-limit: full release and offline recovery need explicit promised-object and promisor-availability checks
